titleStmt.title: Letter from William Tyrrell Jr. to William Tyrrell Sr., 23 December 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Tyrrell Jr.

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: William jr. is busy arranging Christmas for the men at the front. He received a number of Christmas letters from friends at home.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

correspAction.placeName: Ballindoolin House, Ballindoolin, Edenderry, Kings Co., Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
23rd Decr 1917 My dear Daddy, I was so delighted to get
long Xmas letters from you and
Mamy yesterday - The handkerchiefs
are splendid and a thousand
thanks for them. The weather continues just the
same. We are used to it now but
it is very hard to keep one's feet
on the roads. I got a Xmas card from Col McCammond
+ a nice letter from Mrs McCammond.
Also a long letters from Fannie
+ Sissie which I must answer
soon. Meta Miller also sent me
a card. Everyone seems to have
remembered me. I will have dozens of letters to write now but must try to get through with them. There is no news. Our chief
occupation is arranging Xmas for
the men. I think everything will
be successful. We are a very merry party in our
company mess. We have the nicest
officers in the battalion + I know
we are the happiest of all. No more news now and
I may not be able to post a
letter till the 26th so do not
expect me. I will then write a
good one and tell you all about it With very best love to all
Your most affec Son
Willie
